docs.title: Webuzo Documentation & Tutorials | Webuzo Server Management Guides
docs.text:
CORNQ Webuzo Documentation Webuzo Server Setup, Fixes and Troubleshooting Guides

Practical notes for Webuzo administration, OpenLiteSpeed, MySQL, FTP, email delivery, migrations, resource limits, Backuply permissions, and AlmaLinux server setup.

Start troubleshooting Migration guides

docs.title: Popular Guides
docs.text: OLS OpenLiteSpeed Fix Diagnose stopped services, logs, ports, and restarts FTP FTP Passive Ports Fix upload, download, and directory listing issues SMTP MailBaby Setup Configure authenticated outbound email relay SQL MySQL SQL Mode Resolve ONLY_FULL_GROUP_BY compatibility errors

docs.title: Resources
docs.text:
Docs Official Webuzo Docs Vendor reference and admin documentation KB CORNQ Knowledge Base Related hosting and support articles Git Contribute on GitHub Improve or correct these guides Help Submit Support Ticket Get help from CORNQ support

Note

This documentation is continuously updated. Solutions are compiled from support tickets, community contributions, trusted internet sources, and the official Webuzo documentation for reference.

Danger

cornQ is not responsible for any issues or damages caused by applying the instructions in this documentation. Proceed at your own risk.

docs.title: Allow Webuzo Users to Delete Backuply Backup Files
docs.text:
The end user may take a backup in the Webuzo panel, but is unable to delete the file. In this case, the following solution will work.

When the user tries to delete his/her backup, he/she gets the following error.

Run the following command to allow delete permission to the user for his/her backup.

backuply --enable-delete-backups

This will enable the delete option for Backuply in the end user panel.

By default, the backup file deletion for the end user is restricted.

docs.title: Set Up AWStats Cron in Webuzo
docs.text:
You can use the below provided CRON from Admin terminal to update the visitors count without clicking on Update now button from AWStats panel.

/usr/local/apps/perl/bin/perl /usr/local/apps/awstats/tools/awstats_updateall.pl now -awstatsprog=/usr/local/apps/awstats/wwwroot/cgi-bin/awstats.pl -configdir=/usr/local/apps/awstats/wwwroot/cgi-bin/

docs.title: Check Webuzo User Resource Limits from Terminal
docs.text:
By running the following command from the end user terminal, you'll get the resource limit value for that user. 

ulimit -a

docs.title: Disable ONLY_FULL_GROUP_BY in MySQL on Webuzo
docs.text:
Sometimes, we may get the following error, which prevents us from running our web app.

Add/update the following line under the [mysqld] section in /etc/my.cnf and then restart the MySQL service.

SET GLOBAL sql_mode = 'STRICT_TRANS_TABLES,NO_ENGINE_SUBSTITUTION';

docs.title: Clean Email Queue in Webuzo
From panel
docs.text:
Go to Webuzo Admin Panel > Email > Email Queue Manager.

There you can:

  • Select emails and click Delete Selected
  • Or click Delete All to clear the whole queue

docs.title: From command line as root
docs.text:
Run the following CLI command for clearing the email queue.

webuzo --delete_email_queue

Danger

Deleting queue entries is irreversible and cannot be undone!

Running the following command will show how many messages are in the queue.

exim -bpc

docs.title: Enable cGroups v2 in Webuzo on AlmaLinux
docs.text:
Follow this Webuzo official Instruction: How to enable cGroups v2

Tip

If the instruction doesn't work, then follow the same guide and after that execute the following command :

/usr/local/emps/bin/php /usr/local/webuzo/cron.php

docs.title: Enable XFS Quotas for Webuzo on AlmaLinux 9
docs.text:
Follow this Webuzo official Instruction: Enable XFS Quotas

Run command 

nano /etc/sysconfig/grub

Append the rootflags=uquota in GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=" .... " line

GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX="crashkernel=auto rhgb quiet rootflags=uquota"

Danger

Keep previous record too inside GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX= . Don't remove anything without having proper knowledge or replace the whole line

Rebuild the grub configuration file by executing following command.

grub2-mkconfig -o /boot/grub2/grub.cfg

docs.title: Reboot the server
docs.text:
Note

If your OS is almalinux 9 and the above solution did not work, then execute the below command as root and reboot the server:

grubby --args="rootflags=uquota" --update-kernel=ALL

Tip

Make a copy of the grub configuration file.

cp -ax /boot/efi/EFI/almalinux/grub.cfg /boot/efi/EFI/almalinux/grub.cfg.$(date +%s)

docs.title: Fix Webuzo User Directory Permissions
docs.text:
To fix permissions and ownership for all users, execute the following command:

webuzo --fix_user_dir --perms

To fix permissions and ownership for a specific user, execute the following command:

Info

Replace {username} with the user's username.

webuzo --fix_user_dir --users={username}

docs.title: Fix Webuzo FTP Passive Port File Transfer Issues
docs.text:
Add the passive port range 30000-50000 or 30000:50000 to the TCP_IN and TCP_OUT sections in the CSF configuration.

This will solve the issue.

Note

Sometimes, enabling the 30000:50000 port doesn't work. In that case, allow the following port range too.

55000–60000 or 55000:60000

docs.title: Change Webuzo Hostname Default Page
docs.text:
The web page of the hostname of the Webuzo panel shows the server engine's default page, for example, OpenLiteSpeed/LiteSpeed shows its 404 page, nginx shows its default page and Apache its own page.

To customize or change it, follow the instructions.

You can change the default page from the Webuzo admin panel >> Users >> Web Template Editor

Note

There you can write HTML code or upload your own HTML template.

docs.title: Increase SQL Upload Size in Webuzo
docs.text:
Navigate to Home > Settings > Panel Config and increase Webuzo PHP Max Post Size and increase Webuzo PHP Max Upload Size and also increase Webuzo Service Client Max Body Size.

Info

Webuzo PHP Max Post Size is mostly greater than the Webuzo PHP Max Upload Size.

Tip

This method doesn't always work, especially for a huge database. Use mysqldump instead of this method.

docs.title: Increase Terminal Max Processes for a Webuzo User
docs.text:
To optimize system resource limits, please add the following lines to your /etc/security/limits.conf file.

Open the limits configuration file by running:

nano /etc/security/limits.conf

Add the following lines to set the process limits for your user (replace webuzo_user with the actual username):

webuzo_user   soft   nproc   4096
webuzo_user   hard   nproc   4096

Additionally, some systems use configuration files under /etc/security/limits.d/, which may override the settings. Check if the file /etc/security/limits.d/90-nproc.conf exists. If it does, add the same entries for your user there as well:

webuzo_user   soft   nproc   4096
webuzo_user   hard   nproc   4096

This will ensure the process limits are correctly applied.

Info

This works only for Terminal (inside Webuzo). It doesn't work for SSH.

Verify the change running by following the command inside the end user account,

ulimit -u

docs.title: Install PHP Composer Manually in Webuzo
How do I install PHP Composer in Webuzo?
docs.text:
Currently, it's not possible to install the PHP Composer from the App Install function in Webuzo. However, it's possible to install it manually on the server using the following commands.

Step 01: Download the composer's files through the following command,

curl -sS https://getcomposer.org/installer | php

Step 02: Move the downloaded files to the proper place,

mv composer.phar /usr/bin/composer
Step 03: Create symlink

ln -s /usr/bin/composer /etc/alternatives/composer

Verify the installation with:

composer -V

docs.title: Is it safe to install PHP Composer in a shared hosting environment with Webuzo?
docs.text: Yes, Composer is safe to use in a shared hosting environment and is commonly used for managing PHP dependencies.

docs.title: Configure MailBaby SMTP Relay in Webuzo
We tested with MailBaby and it worked fine.
MailBaby Setup
Step 01: Go to Webuzo Admin Panel >> Email >> Mail Settings
Step 02: Navigate Section Header > begin authenticator & Rule > dovecot_login
docs.text:
mailbaby_login:
    driver = plaintext
    public_name = LOGIN
    client_send = : yourusername : yourpassword

Tip

Replace your username and password with the yourusername & yourpassword

docs.title: Step 03: Navigate Section Header > begin routers & Rule > Custom Code below selected section header
docs.text:
send_via_mailbaby:
    driver = manualroute
    domains = ! +local_domains
    ignore_target_hosts = 127.0.0.0/8
    transport = mailbaby_smtp
    route_list = * relay.mailbaby.net::25 randomize byname
    no_more

Tip

Specify your server's open port. Port 25, 465, or 587 are common.

docs.title: Step 04: Navigate Section Header > begin transports & Rule > Custom Code below selected section header
docs.text:
mailbaby_smtp:
    driver = smtp
    hosts_require_auth = *
    tls_tempfail_tryclear = true
    headers_add = X-AuthUser: $authenticated_id
    hosts_try_chunking =
    hosts_try_fastopen =
    dkim_domain = ${lc:${domain:$h_from:}}
    dkim_selector = default
    # dkim_canon = relaxed
    dkim_private_key = /var/webuzo-data/mail/dkim/private/${perl{untaint}{${dkim_domain}}}/default.key

docs.title: Now Save
docs.text:
Note

Step 05: Restart EXIM

docs.title: Enjoy
docs.text:
Note

Official Instruction from Webuzo: How to Add Custom Code to Exim Configuration Offiicial Instruction from Mailbaby: WEBUZO

docs.title: Migrate from CyberPanel to Webuzo
docs.text:
Currently Webuzo doesn't have direct migration system from CyberPanel. They only provide migration from cPanel and DirectAdmin to Webuzo.

Here is the manual migration system from CyberPanel.

Migration From Another Panel to Webuzo

docs.title: Webuzo Mirror Network IP and Hostname List
docs.text:
The Webuzo Mirror Network is a global set of servers that provides fast and reliable access to Webuzo updates and packages. These mirrors are distributed across multiple regions including Germany, Finland, France, the United States, Canada, and India, ensuring low-latency performance for users worldwide.

docs.title: Clear OpenLiteSpeed Cache in Webuzo
docs.text: OpenLiteSpeed cache can be cleared from /usr/local/lsws/cachedata directory manually.

docs.title: Fix OpenLiteSpeed Service Stopped in Webuzo
docs.text: Problem: OpenLiteSpeed service is shown as "stopped" in the Webuzo admin panel, and trying to restart or start it from the panel does not work. This can happen suddenly, possibly due to a configuration change or server issue. Here's how to resolve it using the command line.

docs.title: Steps to Resolve
1. Check Service Status
docs.text:
The first step is to check the status of OpenLiteSpeed. This will tell you if the service is truly stopped or if there's a miscommunication between Webuzo and the actual server service.

systemctl status lsws --no-pager || systemctl status lshttpd --no-pager
  • Explanation: This command checks the status of the OpenLiteSpeed service. Depending on your setup, it could be named lsws or lshttpd. The --no-pager option ensures that the output is shown directly in the terminal, without needing pagination.

docs.title: 2. Test Configuration
docs.text:
Before restarting the service, test the OpenLiteSpeed configuration to ensure there are no errors in the configuration files that could prevent it from starting.

/usr/local/lsws/bin/openlitespeed -t
  • Explanation: This command tests the OpenLiteSpeed configuration for errors. If there’s an issue with the configuration, this will help you identify it before attempting a restart.

docs.title: 3. Check Logs
docs.text:
Next, check the logs for any errors or clues as to why the service isn't starting.

journalctl -u lsws -n 80 --no-pager || journalctl -u lshttpd -n 80 --no-pager
  • Explanation: This command shows the last 80 log entries related to the OpenLiteSpeed service. You can look for any error messages that might explain why the service isn't running.

docs.title: 4. Check for Port Conflicts
docs.text:
Check if any other process is using the default OpenLiteSpeed ports (80, 443, or 7080), as this could prevent OpenLiteSpeed from starting.

ss -ltnp | egrep ':80|:443|:7080'
  • Explanation: This command checks if ports 80, 443, or 7080 are being used by any other processes. If these ports are already in use, you might need to stop the conflicting service or change OpenLiteSpeed's ports.

docs.title: 5. Restart the Service
docs.text:
If everything looks fine, you can now restart the OpenLiteSpeed service using the following command:

systemctl restart lsws || systemctl restart lshttpd
  • Explanation: This command restarts the OpenLiteSpeed service. If lsws doesn’t work, it will try lshttpd. This is necessary because OpenLiteSpeed uses a systemd service, and a manual restart might be needed if Webuzo’s restart button fails to work.

docs.title: Final Notes
docs.text:
If Webuzo’s admin panel fails to restart the OpenLiteSpeed service, running the above commands manually will usually resolve the issue. The problem may occur due to a configuration issue, port conflict, or temporary service miscommunication.

After following these steps, your OpenLiteSpeed service should be up and running again!

docs.title: Migrate Webuzo to Webuzo Using Root Password
docs.text:
Add the passive port range 30000:50000 to the TCP_IN and TCP_OUT sections in the CSF configuration on both servers.

Info

After updating the same in CSF settings, the FTP connection gets successful, and the import will be completed successfully

docs.title: Webuzo Webmail Default Login URL
docs.text:
If you are using a web server's DNS with its nameserver, then the following URL will work to log in to Webmail.

1) https://mail.domain.com 2) https://domain.com:2003/mail 3) https://domain.com/webmail

Note

There is no specific port like cPanel
